etymology.description: 買(買) (mǎi, "buy") + 完(完) (wán, "finish") + 了(了) (le, "completed action") combine to express the completion of a buying action.
etymology.interpretation: 買(買) (mǎi, "buy") indicates the action of purchasing goods, while 完(完) (wán, "finish") suggests that the action reaches its end. 了(了) (le, "completed action") is a particle that marks the completion, indicating finality.

examples.chinese: 她买完了食材(她買完了食材)。
我已经买完了所有需要的东西(我已經買完了所有需要的東西)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ yǐjīng mǎi wán le suǒyǒu xūyào de dōngxī.
examples.english: I have finished buying everything I need.

examples.chinese: 他们不久前就买完了票(他們不久前就買完了票)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen bùjiǔ qián jiù mǎi wán le piào.
examples.english: They finished buying the tickets shortly ago.
